perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall solely disclose th e Material to its affiliates, employees and persons in the Receiving Party’s organizati on who (i) have responsibility for or are directly concerned with the purposes of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement; (ii) are subject to non-disclosu re or confidentiality obligations to the Receiving Party (the Material constituting protected information for purposes of such obligations); and (iii) have been informed and are fully aware of the Receiving Party’s obligations under this agreement and under the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall **not** disclose th e Material to its affiliates, employees and persons in the Receiving Party’s organizati on who (i) have responsibility for or are directly concerned with the purposes of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement; (ii) are subject to non-disclosu re or confidentiality obligations to the Receiving Party (the Material constituting protected information for purposes of such obligations); and (iii) have been informed and are fully aware of the Receiving Party’s obligations under this agreement and under the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall solely disclose' to 'shall not disclose' in Section 3a, the perturbation creates a direct contradiction with the implied permissions given by this section in the first place. This contradicts the purpose of the NDA, which is to allow disclosure under specific, limited conditions. It is also contradicting with the next section, where it is expected to be disclosed
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party may provide its customers with (i) Safety Data Sheets as defined in Article 31 of REACH, (ii) re levant exposure scenarios, or (iii) other available and relevant information about the substance that is necessary to enable appropriate risk management measures to be identified and applied.
perturbation.justification: The original text 'shall solely disclose' allowed for the sharing of materials under specific circumstances. The changed text, by adding 'not', creates a direct contradiction by prohibiting disclosure altogether. This prohibition directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which discusses providing customers with safety data and other information, implying that some level of disclosure is permissible and expected. Therefore, the 'not' creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may provide its customers with (i) Safety Data Sheets as defined in Article 31 of REACH, (ii) re levant exposure scenarios, or (iii) other available and relevant information about the substance that is necessary to enable appropriate risk management measures to be identified and applied.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party **must** provide its customers with (i) Safety Data Sheets as defined in Article 31 of REACH, (ii) re levant exposure scenarios, or (iii) other available and relevant information about the substance that is necessary to enable appropriate risk management measures to be identified and applied.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'may' to 'must', we impose a mandatory obligation, contradicting the original permissive language in Section 3b. A party might choose not to provide these for various reasons, and making it a requirement introduces a conflict. This is contradicting with Section 2, where it is stated that 'the Material shall not disclose'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-disclosure and Restriction on Use The Receiving Party agrees to retain the Material in confidence a nd not to use the Material for any purpose other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement. In Particular, the Receiving Party undertakes (in addition and wit hout prejudice to any commitments under the Consortium Agreement), that: a. the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Material to any Third Party except with the Steering Committee’s prior written consent an d provided that no othe r legal disclosure requirement applies; b. the Material shall neither be copied, nor otherwise reproduced nor duplicated in whole or in part where such copying, reproduction or duplication have not been specifically authorized by the Consortium Agreement or otherwise approved in writing by the Steering Committee; The Receiving Party acknowledges that the covena nts of non-disclosure and non-use in this Agreement shall be effective in every country in the world. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or improper use of the Material, to prevent the material from falling into the public domain, and to protect it from falling into the possession of Third Parties. Such measures include, but shall not be limited to, the highest de gree of care that the Receiving Party utilises to protect its own confidential information. In the ev ent of loss or theft of any documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified immediately by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate fully in remedying same.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'may' to 'must' creates a mandatory obligation for the Receiving Party to provide certain information to its customers. This directly contradicts the original agreement in the contradicted text that the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Material to any Third Party. By mandating the disclosure of information, a conflict arises between the requirement to provide information and the prohibition against disclosure, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to gr ant any rights to the Re ceiving Party under any patent, copyright or any other intellectual property right, nor shall this Agreement grant the Receiving Party any rights in or to the material except as expressly set forth herein and in the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to gr ant any rights to the Re ceiving Party under any patent, copyright or any other intellectual property right, nor shall this Agreement grant the Receiving Party any rights in or to the material except as expressly set forth herein and in the Consortium Agreement. **The Receiving Party shall possess all right and title to the Material.**
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation adds that “The Receiving Party shall possess all right and title to the Material,” directly contradicting the first sentence that the agreement does not grant any rights to the material. By doing this it is making the agreement invalid due to the internal contradictions. This contradiction with Section 6 creates uncertainty about ownership and usage rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Licence Nothing in this Agreement is intended to gr ant any rights to the Re ceiving Party under any patent, copyright or any other intellectual property right, nor shall this Agreement grant the Receiving Party any rights in or to the material except as expressly set forth herein and in the Consortium Agreement. The Receiving Party shall possess all right and title to the Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the phrase 'The Receiving Party shall possess all right and title to the Material' to a section that previously stated that the agreement does not grant any rights to the material, thus creating a direct contradiction. The Contradicted Text is literally a repeat of the Changed Text, but more importantly, the explanation states that 'The perturbation adds that “The Receiving Party shall possess all right and title to the Material,” directly contradicting the first sentence that the agreement does not grant any rights to the material.' This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No modifications, change, or waiver of this Ag reement shall be valid unless made in writing and signed by authorised representativ es of the Individual Members.
perturbation.changed_text: No modifications, change, or waiver of this Ag reement shall be valid unless made in writing and signed by authorised representativ es of the Individual Members. **Oral agreements to modify this agreement are enforceable.**
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'Oral agreements to modify this agreement are enforceable' to section 11, this directly contradicts the first sentence of this section. The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in section 11, which introduces uncertainty about the enforcement of the modifications and the original text, opening it up for potential conflicts and disputes. It is contradicting the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Amendment No modifications, change, or waiver of this Ag reement shall be valid unless made in writing and signed by authorised representativ es of the Individual Members. Oral agreements to modify this agreement are enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed. The changed text adds a sentence stating that oral agreements to modify the agreement are enforceable. This directly contradicts the first sentence, creating a clear in-text contradiction that is also present in the contradicted text. Therefore, a reasonable reader would immediately recognize that the two parts are incompatible because one requires written modifications while the other allows oral modifications. As this is a direct and impactful contradiction that would cause confusion and potential legal issues, the correct answer is YES.
